titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Richard Olney Papers
titlestmt.author: By Annalise Hennessey

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

conventiondeclaration.citation: translation missing: en.enumerations.resource_finding_aid_description_rules.Describing Archives: A Content Standard

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Tuesday March 18, 2025 at 00:27

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

did.abstract: The collection consists of correspondence, writings, original oil paintings, photographs, personal and professional papers, and printed material of American author, editor, and artist Richard Olney.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The collection consists of correspondence, writings, original oil paintings, photographs, personal and professional papers, and printed material of American author, editor, and artist Richard Olney. Correspondents include Sybille Bedford, Julia Child, Alexandre de Lur Saluces, Kermit Lynch, J. D. McClatchy, Byron and James Olney, Aubert de Villaine, and Alice Waters. Writings include annotated drafts of Reflexions and a published version of The French Menu Cookbook , shorter works, recipes, and menus by Olney. The collection also contains wine labels for recipe pairings and a cigar box Olney used to house the labels. Oil paintings consist of a landscape and still lifes, and photographs depict Julia Child, Lulu Peyraud, Olney's artwork, and other subjects. Personal and professional papers primarily contain legal and financial documents. Printed material includes magazines, clippings, and books by other authors.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Elysium Press on the Edwin J. Beinecke Book Fund, 2017.

bioghist.head: Richard Olney (1927-1999)
bioghist.p: Richard Olney was an American painter, cook, wine specialist, food writer, and editor, best known for books on French country cooking that emphasized seasonal menus and wine pairings. Olney was born on April 12, 1927, in Marathon, Iowa. He moved to France in 1951 to pursue a career as an artist, where he befriended and painted fellow expatriates, including James Baldwin. Olney relocated to Solliès-Toucas in Provence in 1962 and began writing about French country cooking. He authored a monthly column, "Un Amèricain (gourmand) à Paris," for Cuisine et Vin de France in the late 1960s. Olney's first cookbook, The French Menu Cookbook , was published in English in 1970, followed by Simple French Food (1974). He also edited the 28-volume Time-Life series The Good Cook (1997-1982). American chef James Beard mentored Olney and Olney's work later influenced restaurateur Alice Waters. Olney died on August 3, 1999, in his home in Solliès-Toucas, France. His memoir, Reflexions , was published posthumously by Brick Tower Press.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Richard Olney Papers. Yale Collection of American Literature, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.

Information included in the Description of Papers note and Collection Contents section is drawn from information supplied with the collection and from an initial survey of the contents. Folder titles appearing in the contents list below are often based on those provided by the creator or previous custodian. Otherwise, folder titles are supplied by staff during initial processing.
This finding aid may be updated periodically to account for new acquisitions to the collection and/or revisions in arrangement and description.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Richard Olney Papers is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into five series: I. Correspondence, 1956-2001. II. Writings, 1912-2006. III. Artwork and Photographs, 1927-1998. IV. Personal and Professional Papers, 1955-2000. V. Printed Material, 1910-2010.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged alphabetically by correspondent, with unidentified correspondence filed at the end of the sequence.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains Richard Olney's correspondence with colleagues, friends, and family, including author Sybille Bedford, chefs Julia Child and Alice Waters, viticulturist Alexandre de Lur Saluces, author and wine importer Kermit Lynch, poet J. D. McClatchy, and winemaker Aubert de Villaine, as well as his brothers Byron and James Olney.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged in four subseries: Books, Shorter Works, Recipes, and Menus.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains drafts, notes, and other materials related to books, articles, essays, recipes, and event menus by Richard Olney.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries contains drafts and notes for the memoir Reflexions and wine history Yquem , and one copy of The French Menu Cookbook by Olney.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries includes notes, drafts, correspondence, and other material related to articles and essays by Olney.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries includes Olney's recipe cards, drafts, and notes, including a cigar box and several wine labels.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Used to hold wine labels for recipe pairings.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries includes event menus and drafts composed by Olney.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged in two subseries: Artwork and Photographs.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series consists of four oil paintings by Richard Olney and photographs of his artwork, family, and friends.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries contains three of Olney's still life oil paintings and one landscape oil painting on stretched canvases.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries includes photographs of Olney's artwork, including a portrait of James Baldwin, and photographs of friends and family, including Julia Child and Lulu Peyraud.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series includes papers such as contracts, royalty statements, and materials pertaining to Richard Olney's copyright infringement case against author Richard Nelson.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged in two subseries: Magazines and Clippings and Books.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series includes magazines and clippings by or about Richard Olney, and books by other authors.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries includes Bon Appetit , Cuisine et Vins et France , and other magazines, as well as clippings from newspapers and magazines.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged alphabetically by author or publisher.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries includes books collected by Olney featuring his own annotations or inscriptions by the author.

did.unittitle: Lionel, Heinic. Si Bandol aoc m'etait conté
Modes de Paris. Les princes de la gastronomie
Pomaine, Edouard de. Le code de la bonne chere
Tante Marie. La veritable cuisine de famille
